style: Dry, medium/full body, medium/high tannin, plum/red-black fruit, oak possible. Softer tannin profile than Cabernet.

conclusions: Drink now to medium-term ageing (5-10 years)

common_student_errors: Calling all plum-driven wines low quality
Missing tannin because it is softer than Cabernet

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Merlot is always simple or soft
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Quality Merlot shows clear structure, complexity, and ageing potential despite softer tannin texture
Plum is a characteristic of Merlot ripeness and terroir, not a quality fault
Merlot can show medium-long finish with good acidity balance despite low tannin

coaching_hints: Compare Merlot's tannin texture to Cabernet — softer, more plush, less astringent.
Does plum fruit indicate ripeness or overripeness?
How does acidity support structure without aggressive tannin?
What indicates ageing potential in this wine?

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Plum and red-black fruit rather than blackcurrant; softer tannin; fuller body
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Plum and cherry dominate over blackcurrant

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Structural seriousness despite softer fruit character
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Pomerol Merlot shows excellent structure despite plush fruit

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Less astringent mouthfeel; smoother, more velvety texture
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Medium tannin feels more integrated than similar Cabernet
